entries.name: Franchise
entries.summary: A franchise is a video entity which can represent a number of video entities, like movies or TV shows. For example, take the fictional franchise "Intergalactic Wars" which represents a collection of movie trilogies, as well as animated and live action TV shows. This entity type was introduced to account for requests by customers such as "Find Intergalactic Wars movies", which would search for all 'Intergalactic Wars' programs of the MOVIE MediaType, rather than attempting to match to a single title.

revisionHistory.summary: New data model format and notation
Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; fixed some defaults; CCB 1823, 1480
New data model format and notation, added FeatureMap, collapsed attribute sets, clarified edge cases around limits, default value of xxxSetpointLimit now respects AbsxxxSetpointLimit
Introduced the LTNE feature and adapted text (spec issue #5778)
Update constraints on local temperature calibration and minimum setpoint deadband and introduced Presets and MatterScheduleConfiguration features
Added comment regarding writing to ControlSequenceOfOperation.
Removed Zigbee related elements and P quality. Deprecated Setback feature and related elements.

entries.name: NoTimeGranularity
entries.summary: This indicates that the node is not currently synchronized with a UTC Time source and its clock is based on the Last Known Good UTC Time only.

entries.name: MinutesGranularity
entries.summary: This indicates the node was synchronized to an upstream source in the past, but sufficient clock drift has occurred such that the clock error is now > 5 seconds.

entries.name: SecondsGranularity
entries.summary: This indicates the node is synchronized to an upstream source using a low resolution protocol. UTC Time is accurate to ± 5 seconds.

entries.name: MillisecondsGranularity
entries.summary: This indicates the node is synchronized to an upstream source using high resolution time-synchronization protocol such as NTP, or has built-in GNSS with some amount of jitter applying its GNSS timestamp. UTC Time is accurate to ± 50 ms.

entries.name: MicrosecondsGranularity
entries.summary: This indicates the node is synchronized to an upstream source using a highly precise time-synchronization protocol such as PTP, or has built-in GNSS. UTC time is accurate to ± 10 μs.

entries.summary: Node rejected the attempt to set the UTC time

entries.summary: Node is not currently synchronized with a UTC Time source.

entries.summary: Node uses an unlisted time source.
